brief_document.title: Extract the worker-harness: provision / warm-build / scoped+full checks / land-via-yg into a reusable module
brief_document.purpose: Lifts the worktree/build/check/land plumbing out of the stacked-dev example so a new agentic family imports the harness rather than re-implementing the live-proven yg/cargo/git invocations (RM-023, S2). Today provision-worktree, warm-build, scoped-checks, full-checks, and land-via-yg are bespoke inside examples/stacked-dev's locals.gleam and worker/handlers.rs; a second family would re-author every CLI call and re-discover every hazard (the commit-before-merge ordering, the merge-from-repo_root requirement, the loud empty-affected-set fallback). This brief moves them — unchanged in behaviour (CN6, P5) — into a single reusable harness module each side. The extraction is a move, not a rewrite: the argv and ordering are byte-identical to the originals, and the WT-001 dogfood arc still runs because nothing about what the CLIs do changes.
brief_document.task: Extract the five harness steps into reusable modules and rewire brief_dev/stacked_dev to import them. SCOPE IN: create src/stacked_dev/harness.gleam holding the lifted provision_worktree, warm_build, scoped_checks (with the affected-set scoping and the loud workspace-wide fallback), full_checks, and land logic that currently lives in locals.gleam; create worker/src/harness.rs as the Rust mirror; rewire locals.gleam and worker/handlers.rs to delegate to the harness modules; add harness_test.gleam pinning each lifted invocation's argv. SCOPE OUT: the agentic locals (scout/dev/dev_review/dev_resume/enrich_brief) stay where WT-001 left them — they are agent-specific, not harness; the control skeleton stays in brief_dev.gleam (WT-003 lifts it). DEPENDENCY: serial after WT-001 (CN10) — this brief edits the same locals.gleam/handlers.rs WT-001 reshaped, so it must follow. STORY SPLIT: S11 (named reusable modules) is shared with WT-003 — this brief delivers the harness module half, WT-003 delivers the pipeline-skeleton half; both are needed for the full 'one place, not N copies' outcome. THE MOVE RULE (CN6, P5): every lifted invocation runs the same CLI with byte-identical argv in the same order as the live-proven original. provision = yg branch add <branch> <base_ref> then yg branch provision <branch> --path <worktree_path>; warm = cargo build (advisory: a failed build forfeits the warm cache as ok:False and never fails the run, but a missing cargo is a loud Terminal failure); scoped = yg graph affected --plain --direct-only <files...> then yg diagnostics check (--package <name>... when the affected set is non-empty, --workspace fallback with a NAMED scope when it is empty — zero checks are never run silently); full = yg diagnostics check --workspace --format json; land = git add -A then git commit -m then yg branch merge <branch> --yes run from repo_root. The reviewer diffs each lifted invocation against the pre-extraction text to confirm the move preserved it. The harness module is a reusable surface (it must not import anything brief_dev-specific): a new family supplies its own workspace/inputs and gets the same proven behaviour.

requirements.title: Lift provision-worktree and warm-build into harness.gleam
requirements.spec: Create src/stacked_dev/harness.gleam and move the provision_worktree and warm_build implementations out of locals.gleam into it, unchanged in behaviour. WHEN harness provision runs for Worktree isolation, THE SYSTEM SHALL invoke yg branch add <branch> <base_ref> in repo_root then yg branch provision <branch> --path <worktree_path> with the worktree_path built as <repo_root>/.yggdrasil-worktrees/<branch> (absolute, never cwd-relative), exactly as the original. WHEN harness warm_build runs, THE SYSTEM SHALL invoke cargo build in workspace.path and record ok from the exit status and duration_ms — a failed build forfeits the cache as ok:False and SHALL NOT fail the run, while a missing cargo executable SHALL be a loud Terminal failure (a broken environment, not a forfeited cache). The Copy/Overlay/Vm isolation seams SHALL stay loud-failing as today. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT alter the argv, the path construction, or the advisory-vs-terminal distinction. locals.gleam delegates provision_workspace and the warm-build arm of startup_task to the harness.
acceptance: src/stacked_dev/harness.gleam exists and exports provision_worktree and warm_build; locals.provision_workspace and the WarmTask arm of locals.startup_task delegate to it.
The yg branch add / yg branch provision argv and the worktree_path construction in harness.gleam are byte-identical to the pre-extraction locals.gleam text (the reviewer diffs them).
harness warm_build records ok:False on a non-zero cargo build exit and does not return an Error; a missing cargo executable returns a Terminal error.
The Copy/Overlay/Vm isolation arms still return a loud Terminal seam error.

requirements.title: Lift scoped-checks and full-checks into harness.gleam
requirements.spec: Move scoped_checks (with check_with) and full_checks out of locals.gleam into src/stacked_dev/harness.gleam, unchanged. WHEN harness scoped_checks runs, THE SYSTEM SHALL compute the affected set via yg graph affected --plain --direct-only <files_touched...> in workspace.path, parse one bare crate name per non-empty trimmed line, and THEN: IF the set is non-empty, run yg diagnostics check --format json --package <name>... over exactly those packages with checked_scope 'affected: <names>'; IF the set is empty, fall back LOUDLY to yg diagnostics check --workspace --format json with a NAMED workspace-wide-fallback scope — zero checks SHALL NOT be run silently. WHEN harness full_checks runs for WorkspaceWide, THE SYSTEM SHALL invoke yg diagnostics check --workspace --format json and map exit zero to GatePass, non-zero to GateFail carrying the output; the AffectedClosure scope stays a loud seam. A yg invocation that cannot run is a Terminal failure carrying diagnostics (CN5 of brief-dev). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT change the verdict mapping, the affected-set parsing, or the named-fallback behaviour. locals.scoped_checks and locals.full_checks delegate to the harness.
acceptance: harness.gleam exports scoped_checks and full_checks; locals.scoped_checks and locals.full_checks delegate to them.
The yg graph affected and yg diagnostics check argv (scoped --package..., empty-set --workspace fallback, full-checks --workspace) are byte-identical to the pre-extraction text.
An empty affected set produces a CheckResult with the named workspace-wide-fallback checked_scope and a real --workspace check run — never zero checks.
full_checks maps exit zero to GatePass and non-zero to GateFail(report) carrying the command output.

requirements.title: Lift land-via-yg into harness.gleam preserving the live-proven hazards
requirements.spec: Move the land implementation out of locals.gleam into src/stacked_dev/harness.gleam, unchanged, preserving the two live-proven hazards verbatim (CN21, C21). WHEN harness land runs, THE SYSTEM SHALL git add -A then git commit -m '<branch>: <summary>' in workspace.path (norn leaves work UNCOMMITTED, and yg branch merge merges committed work only — so landing commits FIRST), then yg branch merge <branch> --yes run from repo_root (the merge removes the branch's worktree, so it MUST run from the main repo, not the worktree it deletes mid-merge). A dev round that changed nothing SHALL fail loudly at git commit ('nothing to commit') — landing a no-op is an error, never a silent empty merge. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT reorder the commit-then-merge sequence, SHALL NOT run the merge from the worktree, and SHALL NOT replace yg branch merge with a manual cherry-pick or git merge. locals.land delegates to the harness.
acceptance: harness.gleam exports land; locals.land delegates to it.
The git add -A / git commit / yg branch merge argv and the repo_root cwd for the merge are byte-identical to the pre-extraction locals.gleam text.
land commits before it merges and runs the merge from repo_root (the reviewer confirms the cwd argument is input.repo_root for the merge and workspace.path for the commit).
A no-op dev round fails at git commit with the loud 'nothing to commit' diagnostic, never a silent empty merge.

requirements.title: Mirror the harness in the Rust worker
requirements.spec: Create examples/stacked-dev/worker/src/harness.rs and move the lifted provision/warm/scoped/full/land invocations out of worker/handlers.rs into it, unchanged, so the deployed worker uses the same reusable harness surface as the local seam. worker/handlers.rs delegates the five harness handlers to harness.rs. THE SYSTEM SHALL keep the Rust invocations byte-identical in argv and order to the pre-extraction handlers.rs (CN6) and SHALL NOT change the advisory-warm / loud-fallback / commit-then-merge-from-repo_root behaviour. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT move the agentic handlers (scout/dev/dev_review/dev_resume/enrich_brief) into harness.rs — they are agent-specific, not harness.
acceptance: worker/src/harness.rs exists and holds the five harness invocations; worker/handlers.rs delegates provision_workspace, warm_build, scoped_checks, full_checks, and land to it.
The Rust yg/cargo/git argv and ordering in harness.rs are byte-identical to the pre-extraction handlers.rs (the reviewer diffs them).
The agentic handlers (scout/dev/dev_review/dev_resume/enrich_brief) remain in handlers.rs, not moved into harness.rs.
cd examples/stacked-dev/worker && cargo build and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings pass clean.

requirements.title: Harness argv-pin tests
requirements.spec: Create test/harness_test.gleam pinning each lifted CLI invocation's argv equal to its pre-extraction value, under the existing PATH-shim seam. WHEN the suite runs, THE SYSTEM SHALL assert: provision logs yg branch add <branch> <base_ref> then yg branch provision <branch> --path <worktree_path>; scoped over a non-empty affected set logs yg diagnostics check --format json --package <name>...; scoped over an empty affected set logs yg diagnostics check --workspace --format json with the named-fallback scope on the result; full logs yg diagnostics check --workspace --format json; land logs git add -A, git commit, then yg branch merge <branch> --yes from repo_root. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT assert on agentic-stage argv here (covered by WT-001) and SHALL NOT run concurrently with gleam builds in the same checkout (the PATH shim is VM-global, brief-dev CN9).
acceptance: harness_test.gleam pins the provision, scoped (both branches), full, and land argv to the byte-identical pre-extraction values.
The empty-affected-set test asserts a real --workspace check ran with the named workspace-wide-fallback scope.
The land test asserts commit precedes merge and the merge ran from repo_root.
cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam test runs harness_test and it passes.

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the behaviour, argv, or ordering of any lifted invocation — extraction is a move, not a rewrite (CN6, P5). A diff that alters a yg/cargo/git argv string is a defect.
SHALL NOT move the agentic locals/handlers (scout/dev/dev_review/dev_resume/enrich_brief) into the harness — they are agent-specific; the harness is the worktree/build/check/land plumbing only.
SHALL NOT lift the brief_dev control skeleton into a pipeline module — that is WT-003; brief_dev.gleam stays a concrete workflow body here.
SHALL NOT touch any OUTER stacked-dev contract beyond delegating its existing harness locals to the new module — provision/gate/review/land argv and sequencing are byte-immutable (CN1).
SHALL NOT reorder land's commit-before-merge or run the merge from the worktree (the live-proven hazards, C21).
SHALL NOT make the harness module import brief_dev-specific types or the facts/render modules — it is a reusable surface a new family imports with its own inputs.
SHALL NOT introduce a default cap, backoff, deadline, or timeout into the harness.
verification: WT-001 has landed (the facts/sealed-payload reshape) before this brief is dispatched (depends_on, CN10).

Diff each lifted invocation (provision, warm, scoped both branches, full, land) in harness.gleam and harness.rs against its pre-extraction text in locals.gleam/handlers.rs: byte-identical argv and ordering (CN6).
grep confirms locals.provision_workspace/scoped_checks/full_checks/land and the WarmTask arm delegate to harness.gleam, and worker/handlers.rs delegates the five to harness.rs.
grep confirms the agentic locals/handlers were NOT moved into the harness modules.
cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam build && gleam test exit 0 (harness_test runs); cd worker && cargo build && cargo test pass.
cargo fmt --check and cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings pass clean; no #[allow]/unwrap/expect/panic; no file over 500 code lines.
bash examples/stacked-dev/scripts/check-schema-drift.sh exits 0 (no schema touched); the WT-001 dogfood arc still runs (the harness move changes nothing the CLIs do).

adrs.title: No backwards compatibility during the build
adrs.decision: Replace, don't add alongside. No compat shims, no zombie code, no #[deprecated] markers. Breaking changes are made cleanly and consumers move forward. Rejected: incremental deprecation cycles — they double the surface under test for an audience of zero.

adrs.title: brief_dev replaces onatopp_dev inside the stacked-dev family
adrs.decision: Evolve in place: onatopp_dev.gleam is deleted and brief_dev.gleam takes its slot as stacked_dev's inner child; the outer arc keeps its live-proven contracts. Rejected: a parallel brief-dev family — two families serving one purpose is the zombie-code pattern ADR-002 prohibits, and the outer arc's provision/gate/review/land contracts took a full dogfood night to prove against real CLIs; duplicating them duplicates that risk.
adrs.quote: so brief Dev replacing on a top Dev in the stacked dev family. I guess that's fine but like I also don't mind sort of holding onto both for the time being

checklist.text: The worktree/build/check/land harness (provision-worktree, warm-build, scoped-checks, full-checks, land-via-yg) lives in a single reusable module (src/stacked_dev/harness.gleam and worker/src/harness.rs) that a new family imports, rather than the bespoke per-family implementations in locals.gleam/handlers.rs.

The lifted harness invocations are byte-identical in argv and order to the live-proven originals: yg branch add/provision, cargo build, yg graph affected --plain --direct-only then yg diagnostics check (scoped --package... and --workspace fallback), and land's git add -A then git commit then yg branch merge <branch> --yes run from repo_root (CN6).

harness_test.gleam pins each lifted CLI invocation's argv equal to its pre-extraction value and asserts the loud-fallback scoping (empty affected set falls back to a named workspace-wide scope, never zero checks).
The land harness preserves the live-proven hazards verbatim: it commits before merge (norn leaves work uncommitted) and runs the merge from repo_root, not the worktree it deletes (CN1/CN6).

stories.text: As a workflow author, I want to import the provision/build/check/land harness instead of re-deriving the yg/cargo/git invocations, so that I inherit the live-proven worktree-and-land behaviour without re-discovering its hazards.
As an AI lead, I want the lifted harness invocations pinned byte-identical to the originals, so that I can trust the extraction is a move and not a silent behaviour change.

As a future maintainer, I want the dev-pipeline skeleton and the worker-harness to live in named reusable modules rather than inlined in one example, so that I can add the next family or fix the harness in one place instead of N copies.

constraints.text: The OUTER stacked-dev contracts are byte-immutable in this cluster: provision_workspace/full_checks(gate)/request_review/land argv and sequencing, the StartupTask/StartupResult startup envelope, the single review_verdict signal, and the brief_dev child workflow type plus its spawn_and_wait shape do not change. They are live-proven (ADR-008); only the INNER brief_dev data flow is reshaped. A diff touching any of those sites is a cluster defect (mirrors brief-dev CN8).

Deterministic workflow code (brief_dev.gleam) decodes ONLY the facts projection and never a full ScoutReport, DevReport, or ReviewReport; full reports cross stage boundaries as opaque sealed aion_flow payloads the workflow holds without opening (ADR-012, engine invariant 2). grep for ScoutReport/DevReport/ReviewReport field access in brief_dev.gleam returns nothing after the reshape.

Prompt rendering and full-report decoding happen on the worker (activity bodies / locals), never in workflow code; brief_dev.gleam does not import stacked_dev/prompts (which is deleted) or stacked_dev/render. The render path is built on aion_kit's template primitive (RM-022), not re-implemented.

The facts projection is the negative space of the pre-reshape in-workflow decode: every field a facts type carries (dev: blocked R# ids, deduplicated changed-file paths; review: drifted R# ids with issues, has-fixes; scout: success only) was a field brief_dev read off a full report before. No new routing signal is invented and none is dropped (P3).

The stage-contract schemas (scout/dev/review report) and the design-system canon they mirror are unchanged — the agents' output is identical (ADR-012 quote); only the consumption point moves. The drift gate (brief-dev CN7) still passes; no file under docs/design-system/ is touched.

The lifted worker-harness (WT-002) runs the same CLIs with byte-identical argv in the same order as the live-proven originals: yg branch add/provision, cargo build, yg graph affected --plain --direct-only then yg diagnostics check (scoped and --workspace), meridian review request <branch> --reviewer... --as Meridian, git add -A then git commit then yg branch merge <branch> --yes from repo_root. Extraction is a move, not a rewrite (P5, CN1).

The dev-pipeline skeleton (WT-003) is parameterised by an explicit PipelineConfig — stage prompts, stage schemas, gate commands, and the required caps/backoff — and bakes no default cap, backoff, deadline, or timeout (ADR-001 spirit, CN2 of brief-dev): every bound is a config or input field.

The norn agent driver is not part of aion_kit and is not lifted into the toolkit (ADR-011): it stays worker-side; the pipeline skeleton and the template parameterise the agent step. aion_kit surface used by this cluster is the opaque payload (seal/raw/peek) and the template/json primitives only.

After WT-001 the TK-002 dogfood completes through real norn end to end with no workflow-process heap exhaustion: dispatched from the roadmap, scouted/developed/verified/reviewed/hardened, and landed on main with the enriched brief riding the merge. The reshape is proven by the run, not only the type-checker (WT-001 verification, goal 3).

The WT briefs are SERIAL within the cluster (WT-001 → WT-002 → WT-003): all three edit examples/stacked-dev (the workflow, the worker, the template) and would collide; parallelism is across clusters, not within this one (RM-023). The whole cluster is blocked on aion_kit (RM-022) landing first.

resolved_context.intention: Authoring the next agentic family should cost a day, not a brief-set. When this is done, brief_dev is the proof of a pattern rather than a pile of bespoke code: the deterministic workflow process holds only the few thin facts it routes on and threads everything else as sealed payloads it never opens, the worktree/build/check/land harness is a module a new family imports instead of re-deriving, and the scout→dev→verify→review→harden skeleton is parameterised by (prompts, schemas, gate commands) so a new family is configuration plus its own prompts and schemas. The dogfood stays the dev process: aion still develops aion through this pipeline, but the pipeline is lighter, replay is cheap, and the class of crash that killed a real run is designed out.

provenance.quote: Authoring the next agentic family should cost a day, not a brief-set. When this is done, brief_dev is the proof of a pattern rather than a pile of bespoke code: the deterministic workflow process hold
